selected_sense_en: a group of people related by blood or marriage who live together or support each other
definition_en: a group of people who are related to each other, especially parents and their children
mnemonic: fam(familiar)+ily → people you know well → 家人
notes: Primary sense for L1. Both active and recognition. Irregular plural families.

selected_sense_en: a father or mother
definition_en: a person's father or mother
notes: Usually used in plural (parents = father and mother). Derived adj. parental is high-value for essays.

selected_sense_en: all people born and living at about the same time
definition_en: all the people of about the same age within a family or a society, considered as a group
mnemonic: gen(birth/origin)+eration → people born in the same era → 一代人
notes: Cross-topic word essential for family and society Task 2 essays. Teach via family context.

selected_sense_en: the legal or formal union of two people as partners
definition_en: the formal union of two people, recognized by law or custom, making them partners in life
notes: Note: the verb form is marry not marriage. Teach collocation: arrange a marriage / delay marriage.

selected_sense_en: the way in which two people are connected and behave toward each other
definition_en: the way in which two or more people or groups feel about and behave toward each other
mnemonic: re+lation+ship → the connection between people → 关系
notes: Cross-topic high-value word. Also used for object relationships (relationship between A and B).

selected_sense_en: a belief or behavior passed down within a group over many generations
definition_en: a belief, custom, or way of doing something that has existed for a long time and is considered part of a group's culture
mnemonic: tradi(pass on)+tion → something passed on → 传统
notes: Derived adj. traditional and adv. traditionally are very high frequency in IELTS essays.

selected_sense_en: the function or position someone has in a particular situation
definition_en: the position or purpose that someone or something has in a particular situation or activity
notes: Polysemous: also means a character in a play or film. Teaching social/family sense here. Avoid questions whose answer is ambiguous between senses.

selected_sense_en: a duty or obligation that you are required to fulfill
definition_en: a duty to deal with or take care of something, so that it is your fault if something goes wrong
mnemonic: re+spons(to respond)+ibility → ability to respond to a duty → 责任
notes: American pronunciation: /rɪˌspɑːnsəˈbɪlɪti/. Very common in Task 2 essays about family education and work.

selected_sense_en: old (used respectfully of people)
definition_en: used to describe people who are old, especially in a polite or respectful way
notes: Also used as noun (the elderly = elderly people). High frequency in ageing/family Task 2 topics. No comparative/superlative inflection; use more elderly.

selected_sense_en: not depending on other people for care or financial support
definition_en: not needing other people to care for you or provide money for you; able to manage on your own
mnemonic: in(not)+dependent → not dependent on others → 独立的
notes: Teach alongside noun independence and antonym dependent. Common in life-stages and family essays.

selected_sense_en: to help someone or provide what they need (emotional or financial)
definition_en: to help someone by showing that you agree with them or care about them, or by giving them money or other help they need
notes: Teaching family/emotional/financial sense. Other senses (support an argument / support a weight) to be introduced at later levels. Noun sense support_n_1 to be added.

selected_sense_en: to look after a child until they are grown up
definition_en: to care for a child until they are an adult, providing food, shelter, education, and emotional guidance
notes: Teaching child-rearing sense only. Other senses (raise money / raise a question / raise prices) must be taught at separate levels. Very polysemous — mandatory human review before scaling.

selected_sense_en: the power to affect what people do or think
definition_en: the power to have an effect on the way someone or something develops, behaves, or thinks
mnemonic: in(into)+flu(flow)+ence → something that flows into you and changes you → 影响
notes: Noun sense primary for L2. Verb form influence_v to be introduced at L3 or later. Adj. influential is recognition vocabulary.

selected_sense_en: a group of people living in the same area or sharing common interests
definition_en: the people who live in one particular area or who are considered as a unit because of their shared interests, background, or nationality
notes: Cross-topic word appearing in family society urban environment and education contexts.

selected_sense_en: a single person considered separately from a group
definition_en: a single human being as distinct from a group
mnemonic: in(not)+divid(divide)+ual → one that cannot be divided → 个人
notes: Also used as adjective (individual choice). Noun sense primary. Cross-topic: society technology education.

selected_sense_en: the customs and beliefs shared by a group of people
definition_en: the customs, arts, social institutions, and achievements of a particular nation, people, or group
notes: Essential word for family and society topics. Derived adj. cultural is very high frequency in IELTS essays.

selected_sense_en: a person's or group's beliefs about what is most important in life (moral values)
definition_en: the moral principles and beliefs, or accepted standards of a person or social group
definition_zh: 价值观；道德标准（常用复数 values）
root_affix: Latin valere (to be strong or worthy)
notes: Teaching moral/social sense (values = beliefs). Polysemous: monetary value and verb to value taught separately. Note: plural values is the common form for this sense.

selected_sense_en: a settled way of thinking or feeling about someone or something
definition_en: a way of thinking or feeling about someone or something, typically one that is reflected in a person's behavior
notes: Common collocation: attitude toward/towards something. American form uses toward (not towards). High frequency in Task 2 arguments.

selected_sense_en: the way a person or animal acts or conducts themselves
definition_en: the way in which a person, animal, or substance acts or conducts themselves in a particular context
notes: American spelling: behavior (British: behaviour). Derived adj: behavioral (American) / behavioural (British). Teach American form only per policy.

selected_sense_en: a serious disagreement or argument between people or groups
definition_en: a serious disagreement or argument between people or groups, especially one that lasts for a long time
notes: Teaching interpersonal/family conflict sense. Armed conflict and geopolitical conflict senses taught at later levels. Stress pattern: CONflict (n.) vs conFLICT (v.) — human review for pronunciation teaching.
